title: The Scorpions Tale

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Classic Homer Simpson catchphrase at start of episode
jokes.punchline: The familiar exclamation itself
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase but completely predictable and cheap - relies entirely on character recognition

jokes.text: Meep, meep.
jokes.setup: Desert setting evokes Road Runner cartoons
jokes.punchline: Direct reference to classic cartoon sound effect
jokes.explanation: Clever cartoon reference that fits the desert setting

jokes.text: Hey, this was one of the planets on Star Trek.
jokes.punchline: Comparing real desert to Star Trek planet
jokes.explanation: Mild pop culture reference, fits Bart's character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: It's so hot I want to wedgie the sun.
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing the sun and threatening childish violence
jokes.explanation: Creative absurd imagery perfectly suited to Bart's character and the situation

jokes.text: I wet my arm-pants.
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'arm-pants' for sleeves
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay but feels slightly forced for Lisa's character

jokes.text: Children, I hope we all have a pleasant and orderly day here at 'Satan's Anvil.'
jokes.setup: Teacher trying to be positive about field trip
jokes.punchline: Calling the desert location 'Satan's Anvil'
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between pleasant demeanor and ominous name, fits teacher character

jokes.text: No one knows why the early settlers called it that, and I suppose we never will.
jokes.punchline: Pretending it's a mystery why it has such an ominous name
jokes.explanation: Solid follow-up to the previous joke, maintaining the ironic tone

jokes.text: Ha! You fall for that every year, Seymour.
jokes.setup: Skinner presumably jumping at rattlesnake warning
jokes.punchline: Revealing this is an annual prank
jokes.explanation: Establishes ongoing dynamic between characters, mildly amusing

jokes.text: And the one year I didn't, I was bitten by an actual rattlesnake.
jokes.setup: Being mocked for falling for prank
jokes.punchline: The one time he ignored warning, real danger occurred
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist that justifies Skinner's paranoia, well-structured

jokes.text: Yes, if I recall, you took a sick day you did not have.
jokes.punchline: Chalmers focusing on administrative violation rather than injury
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Chalmers' bureaucratic priorities over human concern

jokes.text: I was going to lose my foot. / Of which you have two.
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining severity of snake bite
jokes.punchline: Chalmers dismissively noting he has a spare foot
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly cold logic from Chalmers, perfect timing and character consistency

jokes.text: Greetings, desert dwellers. / Git out.
jokes.punchline: Immediate hostile response
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective contrast between politeness and hostility

jokes.text: It's like Grand Central Station around here.
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to busy train station
jokes.explanation: Standard curmudgeon humor, predictable but serviceable

jokes.text: Oh, sure, first they send a boy, then a prairie dog, then they send in them black helicopters.
jokes.setup: Wife suggesting he be nice to Bart
jokes.punchline: Escalating paranoia from boy to government conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from innocent to paranoid, fits desert hermit character

jokes.text: Sir, you have won a National Endowment For The Arts grant for your scrap-metal sculptures. / You'll have to put that check in my cold dead hands.
jokes.punchline: Refusing government money despite being anti-government
jokes.explanation: Good irony of anti-government person winning government arts grant

jokes.text: This park's environment has been preserved free of invasive species, except for one... rock climbers.
jokes.setup: Talking about preserving environment from invasive species
jokes.punchline: Calling humans the invasive species
jokes.explanation: Well-structured misdirection with environmental humor payoff

jokes.text: Uh, my jaw is not a toe-hold. / But if I reach your summit, I'll have climbed rangers on seven continents.
jokes.setup: Climber using ranger's face as climbing hold
jokes.punchline: Treating rangers like mountains to be conquered
jokes.explanation: Creative absurd concept of collecting rangers like mountain peaks

jokes.text: Better... the prospectors left naughty French postcards.
jokes.setup: Bart asking if he found gold
jokes.punchline: Homer finding old porn more valuable than gold
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities, choosing porn over gold

jokes.text: Bart, this is fool's porn. / I'll take it off your hands.
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately wanting them despite Lisa's dismissal
jokes.explanation: Clever 'fool's gold' pun combined with Homer's predictable reaction

jokes.text: C'est si bon. Si bon. Si bon. And... remorse.
jokes.punchline: Immediate post-viewing guilt
jokes.explanation: Good timing on emotional whiplash, fits Homer's childlike psychology

jokes.text: I'm sure there's a correlation, but could there be a... causation?!
jokes.punchline: Excited about scientific method distinction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - getting excited about scientific concepts

jokes.text: Never get off the bus!
jokes.punchline: Extreme overreaction to finding bones
jokes.explanation: Standard Milhouse panic reaction, fits character but not particularly creative

jokes.text: You can't throw me out! I'm not dead yet! That's the deal, man!
jokes.punchline: Appeals to morbid literal interpretation of assisted living
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about assisted living as waiting to die

jokes.text: That's a fish tank. / You're a fish tank.
jokes.setup: Abe complaining TV only has one channel
jokes.punchline: Revealed he's looking at fish tank, then childish 'no you are' response
jokes.explanation: Shows Abe's confusion and childish responses, mildly amusing

jokes.text: That is so hurtful. I wish I was a fish tank. Then I could filter out his nasty words.
jokes.punchline: Taking metaphor literally and extending the fish tank concept
jokes.explanation: Clever extension of the fish tank metaphor to filtering insults

jokes.text: Either the grave or somewhere much worse.
jokes.setup: Wondering where Grandpa will go after being kicked out
jokes.punchline: Implying there's somewhere worse than death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about elderly care, effective misdirection

jokes.text: In my day, all we put on potatoes was pine needles and barber hair.
jokes.setup: Complaining about modern sour cream and chives
jokes.punchline: Claiming they used disgusting non-food items
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd 'back in my day' complaint with gross-out elements

jokes.text: Nose in a funny book, how rude. Ain't you never heard of conversation?
jokes.setup: Lisa reading while he wants to talk
jokes.punchline: Demanding conversation then immediately complaining
jokes.explanation: Classic cranky old person behavior, sets up the pattern

jokes.text: How's it going? / Terrible. I got this new roommate and he won't stop yapping.
jokes.punchline: Immediately complaining about someone who talks too much
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic setup-payoff showing Abe's complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: Here's an experiment for you: Find a man who wants to marry a lady poindexter.
jokes.punchline: Old-fashioned sexist view of intellectual women
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate but dated humor, shows generational divide

jokes.text: Hey, you call that making love? / Homer, I told you he wouldn't sleep through it. / In my day, women didn't make a sound.
jokes.setup: Abe commenting on Homer and Marge's intimate life
jokes.punchline: Abe giving unsolicited critique and generational comparison
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable family situation humor, fits Abe's inappropriate nature

jokes.text: If you were a boy, you'd be a scientist.
jokes.punchline: Homer's casually sexist compliment
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's outdated thinking, character-consistent but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Back off, you mutt. Or I'll make dog-head soup out of you.
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and violent threat to dog
jokes.explanation: Absurd threat that fits Abe's cranky character, specific imagery works

jokes.text: Trying to get out of here before Grampa wakes up.
jokes.punchline: Revealing they're literally fleeing from Abe
jokes.explanation: Relatable family situation, establishes the problem effectively

jokes.text: Go! Save yourselves!
jokes.punchline: Treating cranky grandpa like disaster movie threat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the situation to absurd disaster movie level

jokes.text: Good morning, family. It's such a beautiful day, I thought I'd take my little friend here for a walk.
jokes.punchline: He's suddenly cheerful and wholesome
jokes.explanation: Complete personality reversal from the drug's effect

jokes.text: there's a spring in my slippers and my wonderful son woke me up with a delicious cup of coffee.
jokes.setup: Previously cranky Abe now being cheerful
jokes.punchline: Complete personality reversal from earlier scenes
jokes.explanation: Establishes the dramatic personality change from the drug

jokes.text: What can I do for you? Eat something green? Vote for someone brown?
jokes.setup: Homer being unusually helpful due to the drug
jokes.punchline: His specific offers reveal his typical ignorance
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempts at progressiveness reveal his fundamental ignorance

jokes.text: Here you go, champ. Go buy yourself some rock-and-roll records.
jokes.punchline: Calling them 'rock-and-roll records' like it's the 1950s
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing outdated language, fits Homer's character

jokes.text: To the antique store.
jokes.punchline: Bart knowing records are now antiques
jokes.explanation: Good quick response showing generational awareness gap

jokes.text: Your kisses feel like raw liver.
jokes.punchline: Comparing affectionate kisses to raw meat
jokes.explanation: Vivid gross imagery that's both affectionate and disturbing

jokes.text: We don't know if this... knife is safe. Turns out it's not.
jokes.setup: Abe making point about no guarantees in life
jokes.punchline: Immediately proving his point by cutting himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate demonstration of his philosophical point

jokes.text: Let me live as a happy man for my remaining 30 years.
jokes.setup: Abe asking to stay on the drug
jokes.punchline: Elderly man claiming he has 30 years left
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about life expectancy with touching optimism

jokes.text: I want to take Grampa to have his belly button pierced. I could hang my Purple Heart from it.
jokes.setup: Homer wanting to do young person activities with Abe
jokes.punchline: Abe finding way to make it about his military service
jokes.explanation: Clever combination of modern trend with old-fashioned military pride

jokes.text: Everything everyone just said is either obvious or wrong.
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's typical dismissive superiority
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy line - dismissive and superior

jokes.text: He's casting a pall over this grim dungeon full of losers. Pfft! Dungeon?! We can leave anytime we want.
jokes.setup: Moe describing his bar as grim dungeon
jokes.punchline: Barney protesting while proving the point
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about the bar regulars' sad situation

jokes.text: I was wondering when that guy was gonna state his name and occupation.
jokes.punchline: Moe noting the obvious plot device
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on TV writing conventions

jokes.text: Lisa is irrelevant.
jokes.punchline: Coldly dismissing child as irrelevant
jokes.explanation: Establishes character as callous villain efficiently

jokes.text: World War II? I wasn't born yet. Funny how many Germans say that these days.
jokes.setup: Homer asking what he did during the war
jokes.punchline: Homer's suspicious response to standard answer
jokes.explanation: Historical humor that plays on post-war suspicions

jokes.text: Can't feel my fingers. Feel 'em too much. Erection. Derection. I smell music.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre side effects culminating in synesthesia
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity of drug side effects

jokes.text: But when you're doing it with people you love, you just can't help smiling.
jokes.setup: Series of horrible drug side effects
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly touching sentiment about being with loved ones
jokes.explanation: Unexpected emotional turn that contrasts beautifully with the absurd context

jokes.text: They're just plain better than the greats of my day. God bless them, and their refusal to learn English.
jokes.punchline: Drug-influenced Abe praising modern players despite language barrier
jokes.explanation: Shows drug's effect while maintaining Abe's subtle prejudices

jokes.text: No, robots can develop human feelings. We're graduates of Arizona State.
jokes.punchline: Implying Arizona State graduates are more soulless than robots
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-deprecating humor with perfect economy and structure

jokes.text: No, no. You're Augustus Gloop, the fat German boy. Ja, it is true, I am Gloop.
jokes.punchline: Homer correctly identifying him as different Wonka character
jokes.explanation: Good Wonka reference with character backstory reveal

jokes.text: Being stuck in that tube changed me in so many ways.
jokes.punchline: Traumatic childhood experience explains his evil nature
jokes.explanation: Creative backstory explaining villain motivation through childhood trauma

jokes.text: You sell drugs. Why can't you dress like that? Because I use them, too, idiot.
jokes.punchline: Dealer explaining he's also a user
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation about drug dealer stereotypes

jokes.text: No wonder you've still got pimples at your age. You got all this candy in your desk. They're Tums, for my acid reflux.
jokes.setup: Bart mocking dealer's acne and candy
jokes.punchline: Revealed as antacids for adult health problem
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about adult health problems vs youth assumptions

jokes.text: Even your acid wants out of you.
jokes.punchline: Clever insult about being so unpleasant even stomach acid escapes
jokes.explanation: Exceptionally clever insult with perfect Bart wit and creativity

jokes.text: Accursed tide. Flee from my wrath. No! I've been moistened!
jokes.setup: Burns trying to look intimidating at beach
jokes.punchline: Being defeated by gentle wave
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns melodrama contrasted with pathetic weakness

jokes.text: Don't player hate. I'm just a businessboy.
jokes.punchline: Combining street slang with childish term 'boy'
jokes.explanation: Clever blend of adult business language with child terminology

jokes.text: Wait, I can't go in there grinning like a Swede on payday.
jokes.setup: Abe realizing he needs to hide his happiness
jokes.punchline: Very specific cultural reference for excessive happiness
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific cultural reference that works for the situation

jokes.text: Three dollars for a cup of coffee?! In my day it cost you a dime and they gave you back a quarter.
jokes.setup: Abe pretending to be cranky about prices
jokes.punchline: Claiming prices were not just cheaper but impossibly profitable
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd escalation of 'good old days' complaints with impossible economics

jokes.text: The flower essence must have the side effect of lubricating the eye sockets to the point of popping.
jokes.punchline: Clinical scientific explanation for horrific side effect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - clinical analysis of grotesque situation

jokes.text: At some point, I think we should see a doctor. No, it's cool... it's like we got our own monster.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting medical help for popping eyes
jokes.punchline: Bart thinking it's cool to have grotesque grandfather
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Lisa's concern and Bart's twisted excitement

jokes.text: Which I may have already spent, ironically, on these novelty eyeglasses.
jokes.setup: Bart admits to selling dangerous pills
jokes.punchline: Spent money on fake eyes while causing real eye problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic connection between crime and purchase

jokes.text: Who loves Mommy's eyeballs? Oh, there's a kitty gonna sleep good tonight.
jokes.setup: Cat Lady with popped out eyes
jokes.punchline: Cat treating eyeballs as toys with maternal affection
jokes.explanation: Disturbing but character-appropriate humor with perfect Cat Lady voice

jokes.text: Your boat has you and a lot of people with their eyes falling out.
jokes.setup: Homer saying they're in the same boat
jokes.punchline: Grimly literal description of their different situations
jokes.explanation: Straightforward description humor about their predicament

jokes.text: Walther, when a man puts something on a Frisbee, he should stand by it.
jokes.setup: Trying to convince Walther to honor agreement
jokes.punchline: Invoking sacred code of Frisbee ethics
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Homer logic about Frisbee moral philosophy

jokes.text: As always, the Frisbee is the mirror to the soul.
jokes.punchline: Treating Frisbee as profound philosophical concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the Frisbee philosophy to pseudo-profound levels

jokes.text: No, a cheerful mob. I'd like to have just one day when I don't put a gun to my head.
jokes.setup: Asking if mob outside is angry
jokes.punchline: Cheerful mob is somehow worse, driving him to suicidal thoughts
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expectations with dark humor about business stress

jokes.text: An angry mob? No, a cheerful mob. I'd like to have just one day when I don't put a gun to my head.
jokes.punchline: Executive prefers angry mobs and suicidal ideation as normal
jokes.explanation: Dark corporate humor about preferring violence to happiness

jokes.text: I will have to be content exploiting their fear of losing hair and penis.
jokes.setup: Deciding not to exploit the elderly
jokes.punchline: Admitting to exploiting other common male fears
jokes.explanation: Blunt admission of pharmaceutical marketing tactics

jokes.text: Besides, our generation's got everything under control. To Moe's.
jokes.punchline: Immediately heading to bar to get drunk
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction of claimed responsibility

jokes.text: What are the odds of that much time happening? Pretty close to zero, I'd say.
jokes.setup: Homer planning to pay loan back in three years
jokes.punchline: Treating passage of time as unlikely event
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic treating time itself as improbable

jokes.text: In fact, the only mistake our generation made was creating that generation.
jokes.punchline: Calling their children their only mistake
jokes.explanation: Sharp generational humor with perfect economy

jokes.text: Wow, their 'can-do' will bail out our 'won't-try' every time.
jokes.punchline: Contrasting generations' work ethics succinctly
jokes.explanation: Excellent social commentary with perfect economy and insight

jokes.text: And if you are a fat little boy in a chocolate factory, never try to drink from the river.
jokes.punchline: Inexplicably specific advice about Wonka factory
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the Gloop revelation, though feels somewhat forced

jokes.text: The tube. My God. Every night I see the tube.
jokes.punchline: Revealing ongoing PTSD from childhood trauma
jokes.explanation: Good final callback showing lasting psychological damage from the tube incident

tmdb_overview: During a field trip, Lisa discovers desert water that makes usually combative creatures get along; after getting kicked out of the retirement home, Grampa comes to live with the family.
